primary_variables.matched_definition: Base or amino-acid substitution (for example SNV, point mutation, engineered missense).
evidence: A single point mutation in the dimerization interface of the ULD not only abrogated dimerization in cells but also prevented PKD activation loop phosphorylation upon DAG production.

we introduced the phenylalanine to glutamate point mutation that disrupts the dimer of recombinant C. elegans ULD and ULD–C1a proteins in solution (F59E) into the human PKD1 full-length protein (F104E)

primary_variables.matched_definition: Comparison by phosphorylation, acetylation, ubiquitination, or activation markers.
evidence: The identity of the protein was confirmed by intact mass spectrometry and showed it to be largely devoid of post-translational modifications.
After this the protein was checked by intact mass spectrometry for incorporation of phosphates.
P-PKD1CAT was validated by intact MS (Fig. S3) and shown to be primarily monophosphorylated.
10 μm PKD1CAT in 50 mm Tris, pH 7.5, 150 mm KCl, 1 mm TCEP was incubated with 1 mm ATP and 2 mm MgCl2 at room temperature overnight.

attributes.duration: 100 minutes at room temperature followed by 14 hours at 4 °C
evidence: resuspending the GST-PKD1CAT bound glutathione beads in 20 mM Tris pH 7.5, 150 mM KCl, 1 mM TCEP, 0.25% CHAPS, 10 mM ATP, 20 mM MgCl2, and incubation for 100 minutes at room temperature followed by 14 hours at 4°C.

replicate_hints: The phosphorylation site mapping was performed once for each protein preparation.

evidence: The identity of the protein was confirmed by intact mass spectrometry and showed it to be largely devoid of post-translational modifications. ... After this the protein was checked by intact mass spectrometry for incorporation of phosphates.

base_candidates_ranked.reason: The PRIDE metadata explicitly describes a shotgun proteomics experiment using a Q Exactive HF in data-dependent mode (DDA) with MaxQuant processing. While LFQ is not explicitly mentioned, the setup is standard for label-free DDA.

modifier_candidates_ranked.reason: The primary goal of the MS analysis was phosphopeptide mapping and site identification (Ser738, Ser742) of the PKD1 kinase domain.

trigger_keywords: phosphorylation of serine, threonine and tyrosine
